name: Koala Kruizers
districts_or_customers: Stark County, OH (Canton, Massillon, North Canton, Louisville, Alliance, Waynesburg)
recent_news: Owner Cyndi Morrow profiled in Voyage Ohio (Rising Stars) covering her transition from Akron-Canton Airport charter ops to founding Koala Kruizers; company has expanded from children-only to wheelchair, seniors and pets.
fit_notes: Small NEMT/private transport (~18 vehicles, 20 employees) in NE Ohio. Not a contracted school bus operator — mostly Medicaid NEMT and private pay; weak fit as a school-bus tuck-in but could be a small SPED/NEMT bolt-on if Summit operates NEMT lines in Ohio.

name: Van's Med-Tec Transport & Svc
principals: originally founded by Van Elliott (per source data)
districts_or_customers: Southern Virginia and northern North Carolina
hospitals, nursing/rehab centers, assisted living, dialysis centers
recent_news: Active BBB-listed family-owned NEMT operator since 1989; no public M&A or litigation news found in 2024-2026.
fit_notes: Pure-play NEMT, not a school bus contractor. Family ownership with apparent generational transition (Van -> Chris Elliott) suggests possible succession opportunity, but mission fit is weak for Summit's K-12 platform unless Summit pursues a NEMT/medical adjacency strategy.

name: New Malwa Express Inc
service_types: freight trucking (LTL, FTL, dry van, reefer, flatbed)
districts_or_customers: B2B freight customers across US (MI, TX, OH) and Canada (ON)
recent_news: Operates large freight fleet (~250 tractors, 400 trailers, 300 drivers) across MI, OH, TX and Ontario; USDOT 2256052 active.
fit_notes: NOT a school-bus / student-transport operator — this is a Class 8 freight trucking and cross-border logistics company. Misclassified in source data. Should be removed from Summit's target universe.

name: Peoples Express
districts_or_customers: 28 counties in Western and Central Minnesota
destinations across MN, ND, SD, IA
recent_news: Family-owned (Jan and Mike Roers) since 1991; grew from 2 vans to 40+ across 28-county footprint. No public M&A or litigation news found.
fit_notes: 35-year-old family-owned MN NEMT/disability transport with founders Jan & Mike Roers still in place — strong succession signal. Not a contracted school-bus carrier; primarily NEMT. Limited K-12 fit but a meaningful regional disability/SPED-adjacent platform if Summit expands into NEMT in MN.

name: Hour Transportation
districts_or_customers: Metro Detroit, Ann Arbor, Flint, Grand Rapids, Kalamazoo, Shelby Township
recent_news: Six-branch footprint across southern lower MI; no public M&A or litigation news found. Specializes in Michigan no-fault auto claim transport, a regulated niche.
fit_notes: Small (~20 vehicles, ~15 employees) NEMT operator across southern MI, not a school bus contractor. Founder-led since 1997 suggests possible succession opportunity, but K-12 fit is weak; Michigan no-fault auto claims revenue is a unique (and regulatorily sensitive) book.
